conversation_history.answer: No, I’m not employed right now.
conversation_history.question: Are you currently employed?

conversation_history.answer: Yes. I want to get something steady—maybe warehouse work or cleaning. I don’t need fancy, just a paycheck.
conversation_history.question: Are you planning to work after you are released?

conversation_history.answer: I wasn’t working steady. I did some temp cleaning gigs and odd jobs here and there before I got locked up.
conversation_history.question: Before you entered the facility, were you working?

conversation_history.answer: I’m aiming for around 35–40 hours a week, full-time if possible.
conversation_history.question: Thanks for sharing that. How many hours per week are you hoping to work after your release?

conversation_history.answer: I’d like something steady, maybe warehouse work with forklift certification or a cleaning crew where I can learn a routine and build up some references.
conversation_history.question: Thanks for sharing your target hours. Do you have any specific goals or hopes for the kind of work you’d like after you’re released?

conversation_history.answer: I’d probably look for something new. Going back to my old temp gigs felt unstable. I want a steady place where I can really learn the ropes.
conversation_history.question: Thanks for sharing your goals. Will you be going back to the job you had before entering the facility, or will you look for something new?

conversation_history.answer: I’m thinking about enrolling in a GED prep course first, then maybe a forklift certification class or some basic computer skills workshops.
conversation_history.question: Are you planning to go to school or take classes after your release?

conversation_history.answer: I’m thinking around 10–12 hours a week on the GED prep, then maybe 4–5 hours on the forklift and computer workshops.
conversation_history.question: Thanks for sharing your plans. How many hours per week are you hoping to spend in your GED prep and other courses after release?
What is the highest level of education you have completed?

conversation_history.answer: No, I don’t have any degrees or certifications. I’m not enrolled in any classes or programs here right now.
conversation_history.question: Thank you for sharing your education level. Do you have any degrees or certifications already? If not, are you currently enrolled in any classes or programs here in the facility?

conversation_history.answer: I want to actually finish my GED so I can qualify for better jobs and stay out of trouble. I’d also like to get that forklift certification and some basic computer skills so I can find steady work right away.
conversation_history.question: Thank you for sharing that. Do you have any specific goals or hopes for what you’d like to achieve through your GED prep and other classes?

conversation_history.answer: I’ll probably apply for food stamps and unemployment benefits once I’m out. I’m not sure how much help they’ll be, but I’ll give it a shot.
conversation_history.question: Do you plan to apply for any financial assistance after release, such as food stamps or unemployment benefits?

conversation_history.answer: I want to save enough for first and last month’s rent, get a phone and bus pass, and have a little emergency cash on hand.
conversation_history.question: Thank you for sharing that. Do you have any specific goals for your money after you’re released?

conversation_history.answer: Um, no—I’m not with anyone right now.
conversation_history.question: Thanks for sharing that. Do you have a partner, spouse, or someone you’re in a long-term relationship with?

conversation_history.answer: It’s pretty strained – we don’t talk much. When I get out I’m going to hurt him, I swear. My parents worry a lot and want me to stay on the right path, but we’re not super close.
conversation_history.question: How would you describe your relationship with your parents—would you say it’s a strong, positive relationship?
